steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes economists' prediction that liberalization would help companies raise money, then reports Chen's finding that companies in liberalizing countries received significant investment increases in the three years after liberalization. The blank must capture what Chen's finding suggests.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — empirical evidence was consistent with the scholarly consensus about the consequences of liberalization.: Chen's data (empirical evidence) matched what economists predicted (scholarly consensus) — this fits exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — companies did not benefit from liberalization until at least three years after liberalization occurred.: The passage says benefits appeared in the three years following liberalization, not that companies had to wait three years — this reverses the timing. ✗
  • Choice C — economists who held the standard view of liberalization failed to anticipate some serious negative effects of liberalization.: The passage reports only positive investment increases and no negative effects — this contradicts the text. ✗
  • Choice D — companies in Venezuela experienced a greater increase in investment following liberalization than did companies in Indonesia.: The passage gives no country-by-country comparison; Chen reported averages across 11 countries. ✗
A is correct.
